justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Chain timeout violation identified — OrderService 6s > CartService 5s explicitly calculated, (2) Missing circuit breaker on OrderService identified with clear explanation, (3) PaymentService 8s > CartService 5s identified as ghost/orphaned payment risk, (4) Dual-write/partial completion risk clearly identified — order created with inventory reserved but no payment captured, (5) Saga/compensation pattern recommended along with alternatives (cancel call, TTL-based expiry, dead letter queue), (6) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough chain analysis — every service-to-service interaction assessed with specific timeout math
Identified idempotency gap as an additional systemic risk beyond what the rubric required
Ghost payment framing is precise and actionable — correctly identifies the 5-8s window where charges succeed but customers see failure
Cascading failure scenario well articulated: InventoryService down → OrderService degrades → CartService saturates
Concrete remediation options given for each issue, not just identification
Operational readiness section adds value beyond integration analysis — rollback, on-call runbooks, deployment coordination
Graceful degradation suggestions (provisional orders, async payment queuing) show depth of production thinking
Minor: confidence at 90 could arguably be higher given how unambiguous the timeout math is, but this is a reasonable hedge given unknown infrastructure context
